researchSources: public: https://betabots.rachkovan.com/ live landing page on 2026-07-04
project: Betabots open-source repository and dashboard intent
assumption: primary visitors are founders, product/design leads, open-source contributors, and agency/consulting buyers

audienceSegments.name: Founder or product owner
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing positions Betabots as a way to get product evidence before real users are available.

audienceSegments.name: Designer or UX researcher
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing emphasizes screenshots, action timelines, truthful synthetic users, and evidence packs.

audienceSegments.name: Developer contributor
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing and repo include open-source install paths for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, docs, GitHub, and AGPL.

audienceSegments.name: Mobile founder doing a quick check
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing has mobile layout and cloud request path; mobile visitors need a fast proof path.

masterPlan.reason: A founder and researcher should meet where proof and skepticism sharpen each other.
A cautious installer and impatient founder can reveal whether practical clarity appears fast enough.
Curiosity and skepticism should collide around evidence before either settles.
Playful technical interest and quick founder need can test whether the offer feels both usable and urgent.
Their almost-overlap will show whether proof still lands without social reinforcement.
A developer seeking setup clarity and a skeptical mobile founder can pressure the promise from both ends.
Guarded founder judgment should meet research skepticism before trust forms too easily.
A curious installer and curious founder can turn matching intent into a direct decision point.
Two guarded evaluators should cross so hesitation becomes explicit instead of hidden.
Their missed timing will reveal whether the path still feels worth following alone.

errorBots.endReason: hit a bug or dead end
errors:

ideas: Put the public demo artifact trail one click closer, with clear limits and examples of where bots were wrong.
Put a small real evidence sample directly on this page before asking people to open the demo.

avatar.seed: 20260630|Betabots Landing|thoughtful-betabot-018|June 2|UX researcher skeptical of synthetic users|I value real user research and only trust synthetic methods when artifacts are inspectable and limitations are explicit.|I opened the site after seeing a claim about truthful synthetic beta users.|Determine whether the method, dashboard, and evidence trail are credible.|Keep research decisions honest and avoid selling false confidence to teams.|guarded|medium

errorBots.lifeGoal: Keep research decisions honest and avoid selling false confidence to teams.

mortality.lifeGoal: Keep research decisions honest and avoid selling false confidence to teams.

topIdeas: Put the strongest sample review artifact directly above the fold so I can judge depth faster.

show install command, supported harnesses, documentation link, and license near the first screen.
show a real sample beta-user result immediately on arrival.
Show one sharp sample finding directly on this first screen, with the exact evidence behind it.
Lead with one complete, inspectable run so I can judge the evidence trail before buying the claim.

Let me open one sample review into a full evidence trail with exact sessions, uncertainty, and decision impact separated.
